question: Will "Crimp" be said during the Taipei 101 climb?
description:
Alex Honnold's climb of Taipei 101 is scheduled to air on Netflix on January 23, 2026, 8PM ET. You can read more about that here: https://www.netflix.com/tudum/articles/how-to-watch-alex-honnold-skyscraper-climb-live-netflix

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during the scheduled stream of Skyscraper Live on Netflix. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If prerecorded clips are aired where people are speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Top Esports and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 25 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming secures 2 or more inhibitors than Top Esports in the match.

question: Solana Up or Down - January 24, 11AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the SOL/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Map Handicap: PB (-1.5) vs Yawara Esports (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Yawara Esports and Prison Breakers in the CCT South America Series #7 Playoffs, scheduled for January 22 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Prison Breakers" if Prison Breakers wins 2 or more maps than Yawara Esports in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Yawara Esports".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: Will the South African Reserve Bank decrease the repo rate after the January meeting?
description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the repo rate resulting from the South African Reserve Bank’s January Monetary Policy Committee meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the South African Reserve Bank after its January 29, 2026 policy-setting meeting, as listed on the official South African Reserve Bank meeting schedule: https://www.resbank.co.za/en/home/calendar

This market may resolve as soon as the South African Reserve Bank's statement for their January 29, 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the repo rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

description:

question: Will Elon Musk post <40 tweets from January 24 to January 26, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from January 24 12:00 PM ET to January 26, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

question: Counter-Strike: Gentle Mates vs Team Nemesis (BO3) - IEM Rio: Global Closed Qualifier Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Gentle Mates and Team Nemesis in the IEM Rio: Global Closed Qualifier Playoffs, scheduled for January 22 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Gentle Mates" if Gentle Mates win the match against Team Nemesis.

This market will resolve to "Team Nemesis" if Team Nemesis win the match against Gentle Mates.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

question: Series T1 Rounds Handicap (-5.5) vs Nongshim RedForce (+5.5)
description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between T1 and Nongshim RedForce in the VCT Pacific Kickoff Playoffs, scheduled for January 24 at 3:00AM ET.

If T1 defeats Nongshim RedForce by 6 or more rounds across all Matchs in this match, this market will resolve to "T1".

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Nongshim RedForce".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: De'Aaron Fox: Assists O/U 6.5
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for January 22 at 9:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if De'Aaron Fox records more than 6.5 assists during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if De'Aaron Fox records 6.5 assists or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between 9z and ShindeN in the IEM Rio: Americas Closed Qualifier Playoffs, scheduled for January 22 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "9z" if 9z win the match against ShindeN.

This market will resolve to "ShindeN" if ShindeN win the match against 9z.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - January 23, 12:30PM-12:45PM ET
description:

description:

question: Set 1 Winner: Cilic vs Ruud
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Marin Cilic and Casper Ruud in the Australian Open ATP, scheduled for January 24 2026.
This market will resolve to “Cilic” if Marin Cilic wins the first set. It will resolve to “Ruud” if Casper Ruud wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam match statistics.

description:

question: Will the total number of TSA passengers for January 28 be less than 2,000,000?
description:
This market will resolve to the total number of TSA passengers reported on January 28, 2026. 

If the reported total number of TSA passengers falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve as soon as throughput data becomes available for the listed date. Any revisions published to data for dates December 1, 2025 and onward prior to the release of data for all dates within the listed range will be considered.

question: Hornets vs. Magic: O/U 225.5
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for January 22 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Hornets and Magic combine to score 226 or more points in this game.

If the combined total is less than 226, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Hornets vs. Magic
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for January 22 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Hornets win, the market will resolve to "Hornets".
If the Magic win, the market will resolve to "Magic".

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

question: Will Ryanair say "On-Time" or "On Time" during earnings call?
description:
This market will resolve based on the next earnings announcement of Ryanair currently scheduled to take place on January 26, 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during this event. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A Q&A event will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this event is definitely cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be audio of the event.

question: Map Handicap: HERO (-1.5) vs FURIA (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Heroic and FURIA in the BLAST Bounty Playoffs, scheduled for January 22 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Heroic" if Heroic wins 2 or more maps than FURIA in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FURIA".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Heroic vs FURIA (BO3) - BLAST Bounty Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Heroic and FURIA in the BLAST Bounty Playoffs, scheduled for January 22 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Heroic" if Heroic win the match against FURIA.

This market will resolve to "FURIA" if FURIA win the match against Heroic.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

question: Will Keir Starmer say "Mr. Speaker" 15+ times during the next Prime Minister's Questions?
description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

question: Will Keir Starmer say "Denmark" or "Greenland" during the next Prime Minister's Questions?
description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.
